steps.title: Calculate gallons needed for double coverage
steps.content:
The deck has d square feet. Covering it twice means covering a total area of 2d square feet. Since one gallon covers 250 square feet, the gallons needed are:
$$\frac{2d}{250} = \frac{d}{125}$$

steps.title: Calculate the cost
steps.content:
The cost is the number of gallons times the price per gallon:
$$c = \frac{d}{125} \times 23 = 23\left(\frac{d}{125}\right)$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
